member.name: T:Unity.Transforms.ParentSystem
member.summary: This system maintains parent/child relationships between entities within a transform hierarchy.
member.remarks: The system guarantees the following invariants after each update: * If an entity has the component, it refers to a valid entity. * If an entity has the component, the specified parent entity must have a buffer component, and this entity must be an element of that buffer. * If an entity has the component, it also has the component which refers to the same valid entity. * If an entity does not have the component, it is not a member of any entity's buffer. * If an entity does not have the component, it does not have the component.

member.name: T:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers
member.summary: Helper functions to manipulate and decompose transformation matrices.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.Forward(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@)
member.summary: Computes the "forward" direction in a transformation matrix's reference frame.
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix without shear.
member.returns: A vector pointing in the "forward" direction of 's reference frame. By Unity's convention, this is positive Z axis. This vector does not necessarily have unit length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.Back(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@)
member.summary: Computes the "back" direction in a transformation matrix's reference frame.
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix without shear.
member.returns: A vector pointing in the "back" direction of 's reference frame. By Unity's convention, this is negative Z axis. This vector does not necessarily have unit length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.Up(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@)
member.summary: Computes the "up" direction in a transformation matrix's reference frame.
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix without shear.
member.returns: A vector pointing in the "up" direction of 's reference frame. By Unity's convention, this is positive Y axis. This vector does not necessarily have unit length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.Down(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@)
member.summary: Computes the "down" direction in a transformation matrix's reference frame.
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix without shear.
member.returns: A vector pointing in the "down" direction of 's reference frame. By Unity's convention, this is negative Y axis. This vector does not necessarily have unit length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.Right(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@)
member.summary: Computes the "right" direction in a transformation matrix's reference frame.
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix without shear.
member.returns: A vector pointing in the "right" direction of 's reference frame. By Unity's convention, this is positive X axis. This vector does not necessarily have unit length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.Left(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@)
member.summary: Computes the "left" direction in a transformation matrix's reference frame.
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix without shear.
member.returns: A vector pointing in the "left" direction of 's reference frame. By Unity's convention, this is negative X axis. This vector does not necessarily have unit length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.Translation(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@)
member.summary: Extracts the translation from a transformation matrix
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix without shear.
member.returns: A vector containing the translation applied by the provided transformation matrix.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.Rotation(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@)
member.summary: Extracts the rotation from a transformation matrix
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix without shear.
member.returns: A normalized quaternion containing the rotation applied by the provided transformation matrix.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.Scale(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@)
member.summary: Extracts the scale from a transformation matrix
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix without shear.
member.returns: A vector containing the scale applied by the provided transformation matrix.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.TransformPoint(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@,Unity.Mathematics.float3@)
member.summary: Transforms a 3D point by a 4x4 transformation matrix.
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix.
member.returns: A vector containing the transformed point.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.TransformDirection(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@,Unity.Mathematics.float3@)
member.summary: Transforms a 3D direction by a 4x4 transformation matrix.
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix.
member.param: A vector representing a direction in 3D space. This vector does not need to be normalized.
member.returns: A vector containing the transformed direction. This vector will not necessarily be unit-length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.TransformRotation(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@,Unity.Mathematics.quaternion@)
member.summary: Transforms a 3D rotation by a 4x4 transformation matrix.
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix without shear.
member.param: A quaternion representing a 3D rotation. This quaternion does not need to be normalized.
member.returns: A quaternion containing the transformed rotation. This quaternion will normalized if the input quaternion is normalized.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.InverseTransformPoint(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@,Unity.Mathematics.float3@)
member.summary: Transforms a 3D point by the inverse of a 4x4 transformation matrix.
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix.
member.returns: A vector containing the transformed point.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.InverseTransformDirection(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@,Unity.Mathematics.float3@)
member.summary: Transforms a 3D direction by the inverse of a 4x4 transformation matrix.
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix.
member.param: A vector representing a direction in 3D space. This vector does not need to be normalized.
member.returns: A vector containing the transformed direction. This vector will not necessarily be unit-length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.InverseTransformRotation(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4@,Unity.Mathematics.quaternion@)
member.summary: Transforms a 3D rotation by the inverse of a 4x4 transformation matrix.
member.remarks: This method assumes that is an affine transformation matrix without shear.
member.param: A quaternion representing a 3D rotation. This quaternion does not need to be normalized.
member.returns: A quaternion containing the transformed rotation. This quaternion will be normalized if the input quaternion is normalized.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.TransformHelpers.LookAtRotation(Unity.Mathematics.float3@,Unity.Mathematics.float3,Unity.Mathematics.float3)
member.summary: Computes a rotation so that "forward" points to the target.
member.param: The 3D position of the viewer (the "eye"), in world-space.
The 3D position the viewer wants to rotate to face, in world-space
The direction in world-space to represent the up direction. If you don't want to set a custom value, use `Unity.Mathematics.math.up`.
member.remarks: Note that the viewer's existing orientation is ignored; the quaternion returned by this function should replace the viewer's rotation, not be added to it.
member.returns: A quaternion containing the rotation which would cause a viewer at to face , with the "up" direction in the resulting reference frame corresponding as closely as possible to .

member.summary: Synchronously compute a local-to-world transformation matrix for an entity, using the current values of LocalTransform and PostTransformMatrix for the target entity and its hierarchy ancestors.
member.remarks:
This method is intended for the relatively uncommon cases where an entity's accurate world-space transformation matrix is needed immediately. For example: - When performing a raycast from an entity which may be part of an entity hierarchy, such as the wheel of a car object. The ray origin must be in world-space, but the entity's may be relative to its parent. - When one entity's transform needs to "track" another in world-space, and the targeting entity and/or the targeted entity are in a transform hierarchy. - When an entity's transform is modified in the (after the has updated, but before the runs), this method can be used to compute a new value for the affected entity.
For an entity that is not part of an entity hierarchy, the component already stores the world-space transform (since the two spaces are identical). In his case, reading directly is more efficient than calling this method.
The component also contains a world-space transformation matrix. However, this value may be out of date or invalid; it is only updated when the runs). It may also contain additional offsets applied for graphical smoothing purposes. Therefore, while the component may be useful as a fast approximation when its latency is acceptable, it should not be relied one when an accurate, up-to-date world transform is needed for simulation purposes.
This method's running time scales with the depth of the target is in its hierarchy.
member.param: The entity whose world-space transform should be computed.
If successful, the output world-space transformation matrix will be stored here.
Required to access the current transform values for and its ancestors in the transform hierarchy. This method only requires read-only access.
Required to access the current parent for and its ancestors in the transform hierarchy. This method only requires read-only access.
Required to access the current non-uniform scale values for and its ancestors in the transform hierarchy. This method only requires read-only access.
member.exception: Thrown if or one if its ancestors are invalid, or are missing the required component.

member.name: T:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform
member.summary: Position, rotation and scale of this entity, relative to the parent, or in world space, if no parent exists.
member.remarks: If the entity has a component, LocalTransform is relative to that parent. Otherwise, it is in world space.

member.name: F:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.Position
member.summary: The position of this transform.

member.name: F:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.Scale
member.summary: The uniform scale of this transform.

member.name: F:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.Rotation
member.summary: The rotation of this transform.

member.name: F:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.Identity
member.summary: The identity transform.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.FromMatrix(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4)
member.summary: Returns the Transform equivalent of a float4x4 matrix.
member.remarks: If the input matrix contains non-uniform scale, the largest value will be used. Any shear in the input matrix will be ignored.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.FromMatrixSafe(Unity.Mathematics.float4x4)
member.summary: Returns the Transform equivalent of a float4x4 matrix. Throws and exception if the matrix contains nonuniform scale or shear.
member.remarks: If the input matrix contains non-uniform scale, this will throw an exception. If the input matrix contains shear, this will throw an exception.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.FromPositionRotation(Unity.Mathematics.float3,Unity.Mathematics.quaternion)
member.summary: Returns a Transform initialized with the given position and rotation. Scale will be 1.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.FromPositionRotationScale(Unity.Mathematics.float3,Unity.Mathematics.quaternion,System.Single)
member.summary: Returns a Transform initialized with the given position, rotation and scale.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.FromPosition(Unity.Mathematics.float3)
member.summary: Returns a Transform initialized with the given position. Rotation will be identity, and scale will be 1.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.FromPosition(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Returns a Transform initialized with the given position. Rotation will be identity, and scale will be 1.
member.param: The x coordinate of the position.
The y coordinate of the position.
The z coordinate of the position.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.FromRotation(Unity.Mathematics.quaternion)
member.summary: Returns a Transform initialized with the given rotation. Position will be 0,0,0, and scale will be 1.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.FromScale(System.Single)
member.summary: Returns a Transform initialized with the given scale. Position will be 0,0,0, and rotation will be identity.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.ToString
member.summary: Convert transformation data to a human-readable string
member.returns: The transform value as a human-readable string

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.Right
member.summary: Gets the right vector of unit length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.Up
member.summary: Gets the up vector of unit length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.Forward
member.summary: Gets the forward vector of unit length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.TransformPoint(Unity.Mathematics.float3)
member.summary: Transforms a point by this transform.
member.remarks: To transform a directional vector, use . To apply the inverse transform instead, use .
member.returns: The result of applying this transform to the input point.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.InverseTransformPoint(Unity.Mathematics.float3)
member.summary: Transforms a point by the inverse of this transform.
member.remarks: Throws if the field is zero. To inverse-transform a directional vector, use . To apply the forward transform instead, use .
member.returns: The result of applying the inverse of this transform to the input point.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.TransformDirection(Unity.Mathematics.float3)
member.summary: Transforms a direction by this transform.
member.remarks: To transform a directional vector, use . To apply the inverse transform instead, use .
member.param: The direction vector to be transformed. This vector does not need to be unit-length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.InverseTransformDirection(Unity.Mathematics.float3)
member.summary: Transforms a direction by the inverse of this transform.
member.remarks: To apply a forward transform instead, use . To inverse-transform a point instead, use .

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.TransformRotation(Unity.Mathematics.quaternion)
member.summary: Transforms a rotation by this transform.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.InverseTransformRotation(Unity.Mathematics.quaternion)
member.summary: Transforms a rotation by the inverse of this transform.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.TransformScale(System.Single)
member.summary: Transforms a scale by this transform.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.InverseTransformScale(System.Single)
member.summary: Transforms a scale by the inverse of this transform.
member.remarks: Throws if the field is zero.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.TransformTransform(Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform@)
member.summary: Transforms a Transform by this transform.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.InverseTransformTransform(Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform@)
member.summary: Transforms a by the inverse of this transform.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.Inverse
member.summary: Gets the inverse of this transform.
member.remarks: This method will throw if the field is zero.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.ToMatrix
member.summary: Gets the float4x4 equivalent of this transform.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.ToInverseMatrix
member.summary: Gets the float4x4 equivalent of the inverse of this transform.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.WithPosition(Unity.Mathematics.float3)
member.summary: Gets an identical transform with a new position value.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.WithPosition(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a transform that is identical but with a new position value.
member.param: The x coordinate of the new position.
The y coordinate of the new position.
The z coordinate of the new position.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.WithRotation(Unity.Mathematics.quaternion)
member.summary: Gets an identical transform with a new rotation value.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.WithScale(System.Single)
member.summary: Gets an identical transform with a new scale value.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.Translate(Unity.Mathematics.float3)
member.summary: Translates this transform by the specified vector.
member.remarks: Note that this doesn't modify the original transform. Rather it returns a new one.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.ApplyScale(System.Single)
member.summary: Scales this transform by the specified factor.
member.remarks: Note that this doesn't modify the original transform. Rather it returns a new one.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.Rotate(Unity.Mathematics.quaternion)
member.summary: Rotates this Transform by the specified quaternion.
member.remarks: Note that this doesn't modify the original transform. Rather it returns a new one.
member.param: The rotation quaternion of unit length.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.RotateX(System.Single)
member.summary: Rotates this Transform around the X axis.
member.remarks: Note that this doesn't modify the original transform. Rather it returns a new one.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.RotateY(System.Single)
member.summary: Rotates this Transform around the Y axis.
member.remarks: Note that this doesn't modify the original transform. Rather it returns a new one.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.RotateZ(System.Single)
member.summary: Rotates this Transform around the Z axis.
member.remarks: Note that this doesn't modify the original transform. Rather it returns a new one.

member.name: M:Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform.Equals(Unity.Transforms.LocalTransform@)
member.summary: Checks if a transform has equal position, rotation, and scale to another.
member.returns: Returns true if the position, rotation, and scale are equal.

member.name: T:Unity.Transforms.LocalToWorldSystem
member.summary: This system computes a matrix for each entity
member.remarks:
Entity transformation hierarchies are created using the and components, and maintained by the .
For root-level / world-space entities with no , the can be computed directly from the entity's .
For child entities, each unique hierarchy is traversed recursively, computing each child's by composing its with its parent's world-space transform.

member.name: T:Unity.Transforms.LocalToWorld
member.summary: The local-to-world transformation matrix for an entity
member.remarks:
This matrix is primarily intended for consumption by the rendering systems.
The matrix value is generally updated automatically by based on the entity's .
This component value may be out of date or invalid while the is running; it is only updated when the runs). It may also contain additional offsets applied for graphical smoothing purposes. Therefore, while the component may be useful as a fast approximation of an entity's world-space transformation when its latency is acceptable, it should not be relied one when an accurate, up-to-date world transform is needed for simulation purposes. In those cases, use the method.
If a system writes to this component directly outside of the Entities transform systems using a , will not overwrite this entity's matrix. In this case, the writing system is also responsible for applying the entity's component (if present).

member.name: F:Unity.Transforms.LocalToWorld.Value
member.summary: The transformation matrix

member.name: P:Unity.Transforms.LocalToWorld.Right
member.summary: The "right" vector, in the entity's world-space.

member.name: P:Unity.Transforms.LocalToWorld.Up
member.summary: The "up" vector, in the entity's world-space.

member.name: P:Unity.Transforms.LocalToWorld.Forward
member.summary: The "forward" vector, in the entity's world-space.

member.name: P:Unity.Transforms.LocalToWorld.Position
member.summary: The "entity's" position in world-space.

member.name: P:Unity.Transforms.LocalToWorld.Rotation
member.summary: The "entity's" orientation in world-space.

member.name: T:Unity.Transforms.PostTransformMatrix
member.summary: An optional transformation matrix used to implement non-affine transformation effects such as non-uniform scale.
member.remarks:
If this component is present, it is applied to the entity's matrix by the .
If a system writes to an entity's using a , it is also responsible for applying this matrix if it is present.

member.name: F:Unity.Transforms.PostTransformMatrix.Value
member.summary: The post-transform scale matrix

member.name: T:Unity.Transforms.Parent
member.summary: This component specifies the parent entity in a transform hierarchy.
member.remarks:
If present, this entity's transform is implicitly specified relative to the parent's transform rather than in world-space.
Add or remove this attribute to your code in order to add, change, or remove a parent/child relationship. The corresponding component is automatically added by the .
When adding or modifying this component, add and update the corresponding component.

member.name: F:Unity.Transforms.Parent.Value
member.summary: The parent entity.
member.remarks: This field must refer to a valid entity. Root level entities should not use ; rather, they should not have the component at all.

member.name: T:Unity.Transforms.PreviousParent
member.summary: Utility component used by the to detect changes to an entity's .
member.remarks: The automatically adds and manages this component. You shouldn't add, remove, or modify it in your code.

member.name: F:Unity.Transforms.PreviousParent.Value
member.summary: The previous parent entity

member.name: T:Unity.Transforms.Child
member.summary: Contains a buffer of all elements which have assigned this entity as their .
member.remarks:
The automatically adds and manages this component and its contents. You can read this list, but you shouldn't add or remove buffer elements.
When an entity with this component is destroyed, the will automatically remove the components from each child entity.

member.name: F:Unity.Transforms.Child.Value
member.summary: A child entity

member.name: T:Unity.Transforms.TransformSystemGroup
member.summary: A system group containing systems that process entity transformation data.
member.remarks: This group includes systems that update any entity transformation hierarchies, compute an up-to-date matrix.
